speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Good morrow, neighbor, whither away so fast?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: I promise you I scarcely know myself.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Hear you the news abroad?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Yes, that the King is dead.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Ill news, by ’r Lady. Seldom comes the better.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: I fear, I fear, ’twill prove a giddy world.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Neighbors, God speed.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Give you good morrow, sir.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Doth the news hold of good King Edward’s death?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Ay, sir, it is too true, God help the while.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Then, masters, look to see a troublous world.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: No, no, by God’s good grace, his son shall reign.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Woe to that land that’s governed by a child.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: In him there is a hope of government,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Which, in his nonage, council under him,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: And, in his full and ripened years, himself,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: No doubt shall then, and till then, govern well.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: So stood the state when Henry the Sixth

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Was crowned in Paris but at nine months old.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Stood the state so? No, no, good friends, God wot,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: For then this land was famously enriched

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: With politic grave counsel; then the King

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Had virtuous uncles to protect his Grace.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Why, so hath this, both by his father and mother.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Better it were they all came by his father,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Or by his father there were none at all,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: For emulation who shall now be nearest

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Will touch us all too near if God prevent not.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: O, full of danger is the Duke of Gloucester,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: And the Queen’s sons and brothers haught and proud,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: And were they to be ruled, and not to rule,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: This sickly land might solace as before.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Come, come, we fear the worst. All will be well.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: When clouds are seen, wise men put on their cloaks;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: When great leaves fall, then winter is at hand;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: When the sun sets, who doth not look for night?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Untimely storms makes men expect a dearth.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: All may be well; but if God sort it so,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: ’Tis more than we deserve or I expect.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Truly, the hearts of men are full of fear.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: You cannot reason almost with a man

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: That looks not heavily and full of dread.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Before the days of change, still is it so.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: By a divine instinct, men’s minds mistrust

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: Ensuing danger, as by proof we see

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: The water swell before a boist’rous storm.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: But leave it all to God. Whither away?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: Marry, we were sent for to the Justices.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: And so was I. I’ll bear you company.
